title: BUG: add missing nullptr checks following malloc calls
author: neutrinoceros

comments.author: takluyver
comments.body: Can we use our `emalloc` wrapper function for these?

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body: I tried, but hit compilation errors. Since the pattern of checking explicitly for `NULL` was already used elsewhere I figured I would take the path of least resistance.

comments.author: takluyver
comments.body: I'd be curious about what exactly those errors were. I'd guess there are other places where it's not used just because they predate the utility function & were never cleaned up, or were added by people unaware of it.

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body: Thanks for asking. Trying to document my answer lead me to untangle my own confusion. I think the mistake I made in my previous attempt was to use a Python `import` instead of `cimport` for the wrapper, for which my compiler produced less than helpful error messages, but trying again it looks like it actually works.

comments.author: takluyver
comments.body: Looks good, thanks. Yup, we'll need `cimport` to use `cdef` Cython functions - these are compiled to C functions without a Python wrapper.
